skill.name: biopython-advanced
skill.description: Advanced Biopython modules for motifs, population genetics, sequence utilities, restriction analysis, clustering, and GenomeDiagram visualization; use when you need extended bioinformatics analysis beyond basic sequence I/O and alignment.
skill.summary: Advanced Biopython modules for motifs, population genetics, sequence utilities, restriction analysis...
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 112 potential issues across 4 files (1084 lines). After evaluation, all findings are dismissed as false positives. The external_commands flags correspond to Markdown code block backticks in documentation, not automated shell execution. File system operations are legitimate Biopython data processing examples using open() for bioinformatics I/O. Network references are standard NCBI Entrez API calls required for sequence database access. Weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance flags have no basis in the actual code content. The skill previously passed a dedicated audit with an 86/100 score and Production Ready grade, with all security gates passed.

content.user_title: Perform Advanced Biopython Analysis with Motif and Gene Tools
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics analysis often requires advanced operations beyond basic sequence handling and alignment. This skill provides complete reference code and documentation for motif analysis, restriction enzyme mapping, population genetics, protein analysis, and genome visualization using Biopython.
actual_capabilities: Create and analyze sequence motifs with position weight matrices, consensus sequences, and information content calculations
Identify restriction enzyme cut sites in DNA sequences using comprehensive enzyme databases
Calculate codon usage frequencies, GC content, melting temperature, and molecular weight for sequence analysis
Work with population genetics data including Fst statistics and Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium tests
Generate genome diagrams and visual representations of genomic features using GenomeDiagram
Perform protein property analysis including isoelectric point, instability index, and aromaticity
limitations: Requires existing Biopython (version 1.80+) and NumPy (1.21+) installation in your environment
Does not execute code automatically; generates reference scripts and documentation for manual execution
Population genetics analysis is limited to GenePop file format only
Genome diagram generation requires the optional ReportLab library for PDF output

use_cases.title: Motif Discovery in DNA Sequences
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to find common sequence patterns across multiple DNA sequences to identify transcription factor binding sites and regulatory elements.

use_cases.title: Restriction Enzyme Cloning Planning
use_cases.description: A lab technician needs to identify all EcoRI and BamHI cut sites in a plasmid sequence before planning a cloning experiment.
use_cases.target_user: Lab technician planning DNA cloning experiments

use_cases.title: Population Genetics Analysis
use_cases.description: A population geneticist needs to calculate Fst statistics and test Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium from GenePop formatted data for population diversity studies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Motif Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a motif from these DNA sequences and show the consensus sequence: [SEQUENCES]
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple motif creation from aligned sequences of equal length

prompt_templates.title: Restriction Site Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all restriction sites in this DNA sequence for EcoRI and BamHI enzymes: [SEQUENCE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Enzyme cut site identification in a DNA sequence

prompt_templates.title: Codon Usage Frequency Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the codon usage frequency for this coding sequence. Show each codon with its count and frequency: [CDS_SEQUENCE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Codon frequency analysis from a coding sequence

prompt_templates.title: Genome Feature Visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a genome diagram from this GenBank file. Show CDS features in blue, gene features in light blue, and other features in grey. Output as PDF: [GENBANK_FILE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex genome diagram with color-coded feature tracks

output_examples.input: Show a motif consensus from sequences ATGCATGC, ATGCGTGC, ATGCATGT
output_examples.output: The analysis creates a position frequency matrix showing nucleotide counts at each position. The consensus sequence ATGCATGC is derived from the most common nucleotide at each position, with a degenerate consensus showing ambiguous positions if any variability exists.

output_examples.input: Find EcoRI sites in the sequence GAATTCGCGGAATTC
output_examples.output: EcoRI recognizes the pattern GAATTC. The analysis shows cut positions at bases 1 and 11 in the provided sequence, indicating two restriction sites for this enzyme in the DNA sequence.

output_examples.input: Calculate protein properties for sequence ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY
output_examples.output: The protein analysis returns molecular weight, isoelectric point, amino acid composition percentages, instability index, aromaticity, secondary structure fractions, extinction coefficient, and GRAVY hydropathy score for the provided sequence.

best_practices: Store all task parameters in config/task_config.json and invoke scripts with a single python command for full reproducibility.
Always use encoding=utf-8 for file I/O and ensure_ascii=False for JSON output to preserve non-ASCII characters in sequence data.
Validate input data formats and check for empty results before running statistical analysis or generating visualizations.
anti_patterns: Do not skip pseudocount normalization in motif analysis; using raw counts for position weight matrices produces unreliable statistical results.
Do not assume the standard genetic code for all organisms; select the appropriate translation table for the target species.
Do not stack many CLI flags; keep parameters in config files to maintain clean, documented, and reproducible workflows.

faq.question: What version of Biopython does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires Biopython version 1.80 or higher and NumPy version 1.21 or higher for all advanced modules.

faq.question: Can this skill execute Python scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates reference scripts and documentation for you to follow. You must run the Python scripts manually from the command line.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for population genetics data?
faq.answer: Population genetics analysis uses the GenePop file format. Other population genetics formats are not supported by this skill.

faq.question: Does this skill provide visualization for genome diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes GenomeDiagram examples that generate PDF output. This requires the optional ReportLab library to be installed.

faq.question: How do I run a task after setting up the configuration?
faq.answer: Write the task parameters to config/task_config.json, then run python scripts/task_name.py from the command line in the skill directory.

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and basic Biopython skills?
faq.answer: This skill covers advanced modules like motifs, restriction analysis, population genetics, and genome visualization beyond basic sequence I/O and alignment.
